data.title: S&P U.S. Composite and Services PMI revised down in November
data.description:
• S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI (final estimate): 54.9 vs. 55.3 preliminary estimate and 54.1 in October.
  • Services PMI: 56.1 vs. 57.0 initial print and 55.0 in prior month.
  • Breaking...check back for updates.

data.title: Global Ship Lease announces agreement to acquire four high-reefer ECO container ships
data.description:
• Global Ship Lease (NYSE:GSL) has contracted to purchase four high-reefer ECO-9,115 TEU container ships with an average age of 8.5 years for an aggregate purchase price of $274M.
  • The newly acquired vessels are currently on time charters to a leading liner operator, with varied median firm durations extending for an average of 1.7 years, or up to an average of 5 years if all charterer’s options are exercised.
  • The charters are expected to generate aggregate EBITDA of up to ~$184M.
  • With these additions, the company’s fleet will comprise 72 vessels with a total capacity of 413,183 TEU.
  • The newly acquired vessels are scheduled for phased delivery between December 2024 and January 2025.
  • The company has received in-principle commitments for ten-year financings to be priced at SOFR + 2.50%.
  • The company expects to pay for the ships with a combination of cash-on-hand and debt.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange posts 26% Y/Y rise in November average daily volume
data.description:
• Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) reported a 26% year-over-year increase in its November average daily volume.
  • Energy ADV increased 18% Y/Y for the month.
  • Sugar ADV and Coffee ADV rose 5% from last year, and Cotton ADV by 20%.
  • Financials ADV increased by 43% on a yearly basis.
  • The NYSE Cash Equities ADV rose by 24% and the NYSE Equity Options ADV by 30%.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Safe & Green Holdings shares climb on two new contracts for modular construction projects
data.description:
• Safe & Green Holdings (NASDAQ:SGBX) announced the signing of two significant contracts.
  • The first contract involves the design, fabrication, and delivery of two modular containers totaling 640 square feet, equipped with a fully functional kitchen. The project is scheduled for completion by the end of January 2025.
  • The second contract, encompasses up to 45 modular containers totaling 320 square feet each, designed to house generator systems. The first unit is expected to be completed by March 1, 2025.
  • SGBX shares were up over 11% on Wednesday.  
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hut 8 announces up to $500M at-the-market program, $250M share buybacks
data.description:
• Bitcoin miner Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT) announced on Wednesday an up to $500M at-the-market program and a $250M share buyback program.
  • HUT shares were trading +1.72% Wednesday pre-market at $25.49.
  • The company has entered into a controlled equity offering sales agreement, pursuant to which it may sell shares of common stock from time to time.
  • The net proceeds from the ATM program are intended to be used for growth initiatives, as well as the purchase of bitcoin as a strategic reserve asset.
  • The net proceeds may also be allocated to other capital expenditures and investments, working capital, the repurchase of outstanding securities, the repayment of indebtedness, and other general corporate purposes.
  • For the buybacks, the company may repurchase up to 4.68M shares, representing 5.0% of the current issued and outstanding shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amentum announces U.S. Department of Energy's West Valley deactivation & demolition contract
data.description:
• Amentum (NYSE:AMTM) announced that West Valley Cleanup Alliance, a BWXT Technical Services Group-led joint venture that includes Jacobs Technology, an Amentum company, and Geosyntec Consultants, has been awarded the West Valley Project Phase 1B deactivation & demolition contract.
  • The U.S. Department of Energy single-award IDIQ contract has a maximum value of up to $3B over a 10-year ordering period.
  • Performance of task orders issued before the end of the contract ordering period shall not exceed five years beyond the end of the contract ordering period.
  • The scope of this contract includes disposal of low level, radioactive and transuranic waste; soil remediation; decontamination and decommissioning of underground high level waste tanks, facilities and any material used in connection with the project; and eventual transport of solidified waste to a federal repository for permanent disposal.
  • The West Valley Demonstration Project is an approximate 150-acre area located 35 miles south of Buffalo, New York.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Greif dividend declines by 1.2% to $0.80
data.description:
• Greif (NYSE:GEF.B) declares $0.80/share quarterly dividend, -1.2% decrease from prior dividend of $0.81.
  • Forward yield 4.22%
  • Payable Jan. 1; for shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • See GEF.B Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: NANO Nuclear, DoE ink MoU to site microreactor prototypes at Idaho National Laboratory
data.description:
NANO Nuclear Energy (NASDAQ:NNE) +6.1% pre-market Wednesday after saying it signed a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. Department of Energy to collaborate in evaluating the feasibility of siting and construction of the company's ZEUS and ODIN experimental microreactors at Idaho National Laboratory facilities.

Through the MoU, NANO Nuclear (NNE) said it will work with the DoE and Battelle Energy Alliance, the current operator of Idaho National Laboratory, to progress the development, siting, and eventual testing of the company's microreactor designs.

"This partnership with DoE's Idaho Operations Office allows us to take multiple critical steps toward demonstrating the economic viability and real-world applications of NANO Nuclear’s microreactor technology," said NANO Nuclear (NNE) CEO and Head of Reactor Development James Walker.

data.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson shot and killed outside Manhattan hotel: reports
data.description:
• UnitedHealthcare (NYSE:UNH) CEO Brian Thompson was shot and killed earlier Monday morning outside a midtown Manhattan hotel where he was scheduled to speak at the company's annual investor conference.
  • Thompson was reportedly shot in the chest by a masked man just outside the Hilton Hotel around 646a local time, the New York Post reported. The alleged assailant then fled down 6th Ave. and has yet to be captured.
  • Thompson was at the hotel as the health insurer's annual investor conference was slated to start at 8a.
  • UnitedHeathcare media relations has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha asking for confirmation and comment on the reported killing.
